firms.name: Blackstone
firms.rap: World's largest PE firm. Owns Jersey Mike's, Great Wolf Lodge, Tropical Smoothie. Notorious for sale-leaseback strategies that extract real estate and saddle companies with above-market rents.

firms.name: Apollo Global
firms.rap: Owns Albertsons, Michaels ($5B LBO 2021), PetSmart (minority), and drove Claire's to bankruptcy in 2018. Also entangled in 99 Cents Only and Serta Simmons debt schemes.

firms.name: KKR
firms.rap: Pioneer of the leveraged buyout. Infamous for Toys 'R' Us where 73,000 workers lost jobs with no severance after bankruptcy in 2017. Currently loading Varsity Brands with $2.4B in junk-rated debt.

firms.name: Bain Capital
firms.rap: Infamous for Burger King, Dunkin', Domino's, Toys 'R' Us (co-investor). Pattern of debt-loading, cost-cutting, and quick exits leaving employees and communities behind.

firms.name: Leonard Green & Partners
firms.rap: Directly responsible for Joann collapse (19,000 laid off 2025), Container Store bankruptcy (Dec 2024), and Prospect Medical failure. Serially over-leverages retail and healthcare.

firms.name: Cerberus Capital
firms.rap: Owned Steward Health Care for a decade; exited 2020. Steward then filed for bankruptcy in 2024 with $9B in liabilities, closing hospitals across 8 states. 2,650+ healthcare workers lost jobs.

firms.name: Ares Management
firms.rap: Co-acquired 99 Cents Only via LBO, loaded it with $775M debt, closed 371 stores, laid off 10,800 workers. Also owns Guitar Center post-bankruptcy after converting its own debt to equity.

firms.name: Sycamore Partners
firms.rap: Retail-stripping specialist. Completed Walgreens take-private 2025. Drove Nine West to bankruptcy 2018. Known for rapidly selling assets, closing stores, and cutting workforces post-acquisition.

firms.name: JAB Holding Company
firms.rap: Secretive German family holding group that monopolized coffee and bakery. CEO admitted in 2025 to shrinking Panera portions, raising prices, switching to frozen bread. Two failed Panera IPOs.
firms.notableBk: Two failed Panera IPOs; 5%+ sales decline 2024

firms.name: BC Partners
firms.rap: Owns PetSmart via the largest LBO of 2014 ($8.7B). Controversially transferred Chewy equity outside reach of PetSmart creditors. Also co-owns Guitar Center with Ares.

firms.name: CVC Capital Partners
firms.rap: Majority owner of Petco post-IPO (still controls 60%+). Loaded Petco with $3.3B in debt. Near-bankruptcy warnings in 2023; CEO stepped down March 2024.

firms.name: BDT & MSD Partners
firms.rap: Bought majority of Whataburger in 2019, ending 69 years of family ownership. Texas Monthly 2025 documented quality concerns since PE takeover.

firms.name: Advent International
firms.rap: Bought Serta Simmons in 2012 via $3B LBO. Engaged in controversial 'uptier' debt manipulation. Filed Chapter 11 in Jan 2023 with $1.9B in liabilities. Took a 90-year-old brand to bankruptcy.

firms.name: Vista Equity Partners
firms.rap: Co-acquired Citrix for $16.5B in 2022, immediately laid off 15% of staff (~2,250 people) and discussed raising customer prices 30–40%. Also took Pluralsight private at a major loss.

firms.name: Thoma Bravo
firms.rap: Software-focused PE giant. Took Anaplan private for $10.7B in 2022, immediately laid off hundreds and offshored jobs. Glassdoor: 'cut their way to profitability at any cost.'

firms.name: Hellman & Friedman / Permira
firms.rap: Jointly acquired Zendesk for $10.2B in Nov 2022 — after Zendesk rejected a $17B offer months earlier. Price hikes and product cuts expected as PE exit targets loom.

firms.name: Silver Lake
firms.rap: Acquired Qualtrics for $12.5B in 2023. Experience management platform now focused on PE exit returns. Also holds stakes in Twitter/X and Endeavor.

brands.why: Vista immediately laid off 15% (~2,250 jobs). Discussed raising prices 30–40%. Banks lost $1.3B financing the deal. Employees were locked out of accounts before receiving layoff notices.
Sold to PE after rejecting a $17B offer months earlier. Now private and focused on exit returns. Customers lose public company accountability. Price hikes expected.
Thoma Bravo laid off hundreds immediately and offshored roles. Glassdoor reviews describe 'cutting 80% of the company.' PE managing for EBITDA exit, not product quality.
Taken private from SAP. All PE exits require price hikes or cuts to generate returns. Customer data inside PE-owned platforms faces monetization pressure.
Thoma Bravo took SolarWinds private in 2016 then re-IPO'd it. During PE ownership, the 2020 cyberattack hit 18,000+ organizations. PE cost-cutting on security contributed.
Blackstone closed Jan 2025. Social media already documents portion reductions. Classic early-stage PE quality extraction — typically manifests in 2–3 years.
Sold by Dobson family after 69 years. Texas Monthly 2025 documented quality concerns. PE expands rapidly on debt then cuts quality to service it.
Acquired by Blackstone June 2024. Franchise fee pressure and cost-cutting typically follow within 2–3 years of PE acquisition.
Apollo portfolio company. PE-owned chains typically cut ingredient quality to improve EBITDA for exit. Chipotle remains founder-culture focused.
PE-engineered sale-leaseback stripped real estate and raised rents above market. 100 locations closed, 36,000 jobs affected in 2024.
One of 10 PE-backed restaurant chains that went bankrupt in 2024. PE debt made adapting to post-COVID dining shifts impossible.
JAB's CEO admitted in 2025: smaller portions, higher prices, lower-quality ingredients. Switching to frozen bread — ending the fresh-baked identity. Two failed IPOs.
JAB acquired 2016, re-IPO'd 2021 but retains majority control. Rapid expansion to 14,100+ locations diluted the quality that built the brand.
JAB's first coffee monopoly acquisition. Once independent craft brand now part of a $120B extraction machine. Sourcing subordinated to margin targets.
JAB converted 88 Caribou locations to Peet's post-acquisition. Classic PE: eliminate redundancy, extract brand equity, standardize at lowest cost.
JAB paid $374M to take Einstein Noah private in 2014, then absorbed into Panera Brands 2021. Your dollar funds JAB's full extraction machine.
Part of JAB's massive fast-casual monopoly. UK staff cuts followed. Prices have risen significantly post-acquisition.
Apollo's $5B buyout loaded Michaels with debt. Preparing for PE exit via re-IPO or sale, prioritizing exit returns over investment.
Sycamore is a retail-stripping specialist. Closed hundreds of Staples locations and cut workforce repeatedly since 2017.
Sycamore portfolio company. Multiple waves of closures and staff reductions since acquisition.
Apollo drove Claire's to Chapter 11 in 2018. Emerged still burdened with debt. Textbook PE extraction of a teen-focused retailer.
Filed bankruptcy 2020 after years of PE debt-loading. Now owned by former lenders who converted debt to equity. Sweetwater is the independent gold standard.
KKR's third PE owner in a decade. $2.4B in junk-rated debt at acquisition. School athletic programs ultimately absorb higher costs.
Leonard Green drove it to bankruptcy December 2024 — same firm that bankrupted Joann and contributed to Prospect Medical's collapse.
Sycamore completed take-private 2025. Retail-stripping specialist. Mass pharmacy closures expected, reducing access in rural and low-income communities.
Apollo took ADT private for $6.9B in 2016, restructured, re-IPO'd 2018. Workers and customers bore debt costs. Apollo retains partial stake.
German PE Aurelius acquired 2023, shut all US stores by early 2024. Bankruptcy filed 2024. A brand built on ethics had its ethics immediately put up for sale.
BC Partners' $8.7B LBO was largest of 2014. Controversially transferred Chewy equity outside reach of PetSmart creditors. Apollo added minority stake 2023.
CVC loaded Petco with $3.3B in debt. Near-bankruptcy warnings 2023. CEO resigned March 2024. Still majority PE-controlled despite IPO.
Advent's $3B LBO in 2012 ended in Chapter 11 Jan 2023 with $1.9B liabilities. Debt manipulation schemes bankrupted a 90-year-old American brand.
Acquired by Serta Simmons 2018, swept into Advent's bankruptcy Jan 2023 despite being branded as a 'disruptor' mattress line.
Blackstone portfolio company. Premium pricing with ongoing margin extraction via real estate and PE fee strategies.
Apollo owned until 2020 COVID bankruptcy. PE debt burden prevented surviving the economic shock. Classic: load debt, company can't absorb a crisis, workers pay.
